data.title: Why AMD Stock Slipped Today and Why One Investor Says 'Buy the Dip'
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) shares slipped 3% in after-hours trading Wednesday after Nvidia and Meta Platforms unveiled an expanded, multiyear AI infrastructure partnership. The deal, which leans heavily on Nvidia's GPUs, CPUs, and networking technology to power Meta's next-gen data centers, could narrow AMD's chances of carving out a larger foothold within Meta's AI infrastructure buildout.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, while investors fixate on who wins the latest mega-deal, AMD's leadership argues the bigger story is the pace of expansion across the AI market. CEO Dr. Lisa Su projects a 35% CAGR over the next three to five years, fueled by what she calls "accelerating AI momentum," suggesting the opportunity could be large enough to drive meaningful gains for AMD.

That growth thesis is precisely why some investors are turning more constructive. While AMD has long participated in the AI narrative, investor Louis Gerard contends the company is entering a more advantageous phase, pointing to its "successful pivot toward a system-scale model."

"I believe that the company is at an inflection point as AMD shifted from selling individual chips to now delivering integrated rack-scale systems," explains the 5-star investor.

Gerard notes that the company's data center revenues have been growing, and now make up the majority of AMD's sales. This momentum seems likely to continue going forward.

The investor is particularly enthusiastic about AMD's upcoming MI450 series, expected for the second half of the year. He calls this a "pivotal moment for the industry," as its increasing memory and bandwidth-first approach is geared toward addressing some of the key hurdles in LLM training and inference.

"That could position AMD to capture a significant share of the next-gen AI superclusters," adds Gerard.

The sliding share price has made AMD a cheaper buy, though with a P/E in the high forties, it's not exactly in bargain territory. Still, it's also at the cheapest levels since the AI boom started.

"With the growth prospects in consideration, I believe that the company is undervalued, especially given the bullishness of the data center segment that could see its total addressable market reach $1 trillion by 2030," says Gerard, who is upgrading AMD to a Strong Buy. (To watch Louis Gerard's track record, click here)

There's little objection on Wall Street to this viewpoint. AMD stock commands 24 Buy ratings against 8 Holds, earning AMD a Strong Buy consensus. And the conviction isn't subtle – the $286.80 average price target implies upside of ~41% from current levels. (See AMD stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Slip Slightly after a Muted Trading Session
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down marginally on Tuesday evening after a quiet day on Wall Street. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down 0.15%, 0.09%, and 0.09%, respectively, at 6:20 p.m. EST on February 17.

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall as AI Disruption Fears Continue to Drag Down Tech Stocks
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Tuesday morning, kicking off a holiday-shortened trading week in the red amid continued fears of artificial intelligence (AI) disrupting software and other businesses. Meanwhile, traders await key employment figures, FOMC minutes, and delayed economic reports. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.79%, 0.38%, and 0.15%, respectively, at 8:36 a.m. EST on February 17.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Last week, all three major indexes closed lower. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones each dropped more than 1%, while the Nasdaq Composite fell over 2%, marking its fifth straight week of losses. Ongoing concerns about AI disrupting software, real estate, and financial services weighed on investor sentiment.

Turning to trending tickers, shares of health tech giant Masimo (MASI) rallied more than 34% in pre-market trading following reports that the company was nearing a deal with Danaher (DHR) to be acquired for about $10 billion in cash. Danaher stock declined about 6% on the news. Meanwhile, Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) stock jumped 6% following a Wall Street Journal report that Elliott Investment Management has built a more than 10% stake in the company.

Also, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) stock rose about 3% after Netflix (NFLX) granted Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) a seven-day waiver to hold deal talks with Paramount. WBD stock gained 2% on the news.

data.title: How Rivian Automotive (RIVN) Proved Its Doubters Wrong
data.description:
After a correction that began in mid-December, following significant gains in the prior period, Rivian Automotive (RIVN) stock rose by double-digits again after the company reported its Q4 results last week. If the earlier rally was driven by the announcement of its in-house chip, AI strategy, and monetization initiatives, this time the catalyst was different: a growing perception that scaling is becoming increasingly viable ahead of the launch of the more affordable R2, coupled with a reduced sense of liquidity risk.

data.title: Why Is Applied Digital Stock (APLD) Down Today and What Northland Expects Next
data.description:
Applied Digital (NASDAQ:APLD) shares tumbled 7.5% in after-hours trading Wednesday after Nvidia's latest filing revealed it had exited its roughly $177 million stake in the digital infrastructure company. The development caught investors off guard, as Nvidia had previously been viewed as a strategic backer, and its decision to sell naturally raised questions about whether anything fundamental had changed in the story.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Applied Digital has been positioning itself as a large-scale data center and high-performance computing infrastructure provider, targeting hyperscale customers that require significant capacity to support AI workloads. Management has repeatedly pointed to a growing pipeline of advanced-stage negotiations, pre-leasing activity, and expanding development capacity as core drivers of future revenue.

While the market reaction focused on Nvidia's ownership decision, Northland analyst Mike Grondahl sees the bigger picture differently. Grondahl argues that Applied Digital is "strategically positioned to outperform its peers in 2026," pointing to its more than 9 gigawatts of development pipeline, including 4.3 gigawatts already under active development.

A key element of the thesis is Applied Digital's $5 billion Macquarie Asset Management facility, which Grondahl says effectively unlocks $20–$25 billion in total capital. That funding structure, in the analyst's view, allows the company to finance and expand multiple AI campuses with minimal dilution, a crucial factor in a capital-intensive industry.

Grondahl also highlights the company's ~$100 million of pre-lease financing for new data center projects that are currently in advanced-stage negotiations with a new investment-grade hyperscaler. Applied Digital is in discussions with all U.S.-based investment-grade hyperscalers, with several described as increasingly aggressive, and has already entered negotiations with two new hyperscalers for new sites.

Execution, according to Grondahl, is supported by the successful delivery of the first 100 megawatts for FP1 and the company's multi-year history of building and operating BTC mining facilities in the region. The analyst adds that execution risk is further mitigated by Applied Digital's modular, off-site manufacturing process, which allows facilities to be built and deployed efficiently, alongside the fact that required equipment has already been ordered and much of the labor secured.

Looking ahead, Grondahl believes Applied Digital's goal of approximately $1 billion in contracted annual NOI is achievable in 2026, citing detailed visibility into the timing and monetization of its pipeline, including 4.3 gigawatts in advanced development.

Reflecting that confidence, the analyst assigns APLD shares an Outperform rating and a 12-month price target of $56, implying 67% upside from current levels. (To watch Grondahl's track record, click here)

Elsewhere on the Street, sentiment remains firmly constructive. APLD carries a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 10 recent analyst reviews, all of which are Buys. The average 12-month price target stands at $52.22, implying ~56% upside from Wednesday's closing price of $33.56. (See APLD stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: South China Sea Tensions Rise – Is It Time to Buy a Defense ETF?
data.description:
The United States is planning to send more advanced missile systems to the Philippines as tensions continue to rise in the South China Sea. During recent annual security talks in Manila, both governments strongly criticized what they described as China's "illegal, coercive, aggressive, and deceptive activities" in the region. As a result, the two allies laid out plans to increase their military cooperation this year. This includes more joint military exercises, U.S. support to modernize the Philippine armed forces, and increased deployments of advanced missile and unmanned systems.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Unsurprisingly, China has strongly opposed these deployments and previously criticized the U.S. Army's decision to deploy the Typhon mid-range missile system to northern Luzon in April 2024, as well as the Navy Marine Expeditionary Ship Interdiction System deployed there last year. Chinese officials argue that these weapons are meant to contain China's rise and threaten regional stability. They have also formally asked the Philippines to remove the missile systems. However, officials under President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. have rejected that request.

In fact, U.S. and Philippine defense leaders are now discussing the possible deployment of upgraded missile systems that the Philippines could eventually decide to purchase for itself. Importantly, the Typhon launcher can fire Tomahawk Land Attack Missiles that travel more than 1,000 miles, which puts parts of China within range from northern Luzon. For investors who are looking for exposure to defense stocks, available funds include the iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA), the SPDR S&P Aerospace & Defense ETF (XAR), and the Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF (PPA), all of which have Buy ratings from analysts.

Which Defense ETF is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three defense ETFs mentioned above, analysts think that XAR has the most room to run. In fact, XAR's price target of $315.24 per share implies 15.3% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Meta Platforms (META) Taps Nvidia (NVDA) for Millions of New AI Chips in Major Multi-Year Deal
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) and Meta Platforms (META) have expanded their long‑running partnership with a new multi‑year deal that will bring millions of Nvidia's latest AI chips into Meta's data centers. The move supports Meta's push to scale its AI infrastructure for everything from recommendations to next‑generation "personal superintelligence."

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The announcement led to a rise in both Nvidia and Meta stocks in after-hours trading. Meanwhile, shares of rivals AMD (AMD) and Arista Networks (ANET) declined as the deal solidifies NVDA's dominance in Meta's massive AI spending roadmap.

Millions of New GPUs and CPUs Heading to Meta

Under the expanded deal, Nvidia will supply Meta with millions of its Blackwell and Rubin GPUs, along with its Grace CPUs and advanced networking hardware. Meta plans to use the chips across its U.S. data centers for both training and running AI models.

The partnership also includes Meta's first large-scale deployment of Nvidia's Grace CPU-only servers, with plans to adopt next-generation Vera CPU-only systems in 2027. This marks an important shift that could pressure long-time CPU incumbents Intel (INTC) and AMD.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang emphasized Meta's unique scale in deploying AI across research and production systems that serve billions of users. He highlighted the companies' "deep codesign" across GPUs, CPUs, networking, and software as they work together to build the next frontier of AI infrastructure.

Beyond data center expansion, Meta will also adopt Nvidia's Confidential Computing technology to enable secure, private data processing on WhatsApp, with plans to expand across its other platforms.

Is Nvidia Stock a Good Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 37 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations. The average NVDA stock price target of $260.38 indicates 40.77% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: "46% Upside Potential": Here's Why This Top Analyst Is Bullish on Nvidia Stock (NVDA) ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Citi analyst Atif Malik is bullish on Nvidia (NVDA) stock ahead of its Q4 earnings report on February 25, pointing to strong product momentum and improving demand visibility that stretches well into 2027. He reiterated a Buy rating on NVDA stock and a $270 price target, which implies 46% upside potential.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The five-star analyst expects Nvidia to deliver another standout quarter, projecting January‑quarter revenue of $67 billion, comfortably above the Street's consensus of $65.6 billion. He also anticipates April‑quarter sales guidance of $73 billion, topping analyst estimates of $71.6 billion.

Analyst Sees Stronger Growth Ahead

Malik's optimism is driven by Nvidia's continued ramp of its B300 and Rubin platforms, which he believes will fuel a 34% acceleration in the second half of 2026 sales, compared with 27% growth in the first half.

Malik also forecasts Fiscal 2027 gross margins of around 75%. Moreover, he expects operating expense growth to remain in the high‑30% range, similar to Fiscal 2026.

He noted that most investors are already looking beyond the quarter toward Nvidia's annual GTC conference in mid-March, where the company is expected to outline its next-generation inference roadmap, including the use of Groq's low-latency SRAM IP.

Further, the analyst believes hyperscaler spending, while a concern for some investors, will ultimately deliver long-term returns as AI-driven infrastructure demand accelerates cloud revenue growth.

NVDA's Q4 Earnings and Revenue Expected to Jump Y/Y

Currently, analysts expect NVDA to post revenue of $65.58 billion in Q4, up from $39.33 billion in the year-ago quarter. Further, the company is expected to report earnings of $1.52 per share, compared with earnings of $0.89 in the prior-year quarter.

The market will be looking for updates on the rollout of Nvidia's latest platforms and any early signals about its 2026 product roadmap. Also, the company's guidance will play a major role in shaping sentiments around the broader AI and semiconductor sectors.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 379 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations. The average NVDA stock price target of $260.38 indicates 40.77% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Dan Ives Pounds the Table on These 3 Cyber Security Stocks
data.description:
Cyber security stocks have struggled in 2026 due to the AI-driven sell-off in software. However, five-star Wedbush analyst Dan Ives believes that this weakness could present a buying opportunity. In a recent note, he argued that artificial intelligence will ultimately act as a powerful tailwind for the sector. This is because as AI adoption expands across businesses, so does the need to protect data, applications, and endpoints.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, that view is based on the simple reality that hackers are also using AI, which is forcing companies to spend more on defense. According to Wedbush, some security vendors are actually raising their sales targets by as much as 30% this year due to strong demand. Even though IT budgets may face pressure, security spending is increasingly seen as non-negotiable. Still, bears argue that rising AI costs could crowd out other IT spending, thereby limiting overall growth. Nevertheless, Ives highlighted three companies that he believes are best positioned to benefit:

  • CrowdStrike (CRWD)
  • Palo Alto Networks (PANW)
  • Zscaler (ZS)

Notably, he called CrowdStrike the industry's "gold standard" due to the strength of its Falcon platform, and maintained a Buy rating with a $600 price target. Secondly, Ives said that Palo Alto Networks remains a top pick, thanks to its platform consolidation strategy and its CyberArk acquisition, which earned the stock a $225 target. Finally, Zscaler received a $350 price target from Ives as a result of its durable subscription growth tied to its Zero Trust model.

Which Cyber Security Stock Is the Better Buy?

Overall, out of the three stocks mentioned above, analysts think that ZS stock has the most room to run. In fact, Zscaler's average price target of $315.24 per share implies 82.7% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
